locked_facts.terminal_voltage_formula: V = EMF - I*r (terminal voltage equals EMF minus internal voltage drop)
locked_facts.short_circuit_current: I_short = EMF / r (maximum current when terminals are short-circuited)

ncert_truth_statements: The terminal voltage of a cell is given by V = EMF - Ir, where r is the internal resistance of the cell
When current flows through a cell, the terminal voltage is less than the EMF due to the internal resistance drop Ir
The internal resistance r arises from the resistance to current flow within the electrolyte of the cell
The short-circuit current (maximum current) equals EMF divided by the internal resistance: I = EMF/r
When no current is drawn (open circuit), the terminal voltage equals the EMF of the cell
